# Copilot Working Notes

- Architecture: core PyTorch training/inference code in [src](src); single-file FastAPI + WebSocket API in [app.py](app.py); modular FastAPI backend in [web-app/backend/app](web-app/backend/app/main.py) that reuses the same model via [model_service](web-app/backend/app/services/model_service.py); React/Vite UI in [web-app/frontend](web-app/frontend/src/App.jsx); Docker Compose wrappers in [web-app](web-app).
- Models: main architecture in [src/models/dehazenet.py](src/models/dehazenet.py); pretrained weights live in [models/pretrained](models/pretrained) with names `dehazenet_{layers}layers_best.pth`. Backends auto-fall back to CPU if CUDA is unavailable.
- Config: Python CLI defaults in [config/config.py](config/config.py); single-file API env via `.env` read by [app.py](app.py); modular backend settings in [web-app/backend/app/core/config.py](web-app/backend/app/core/config.py) (creates uploads/outputs on init, parses comma-separated env lists).
- CLI inference (side-by-side output): from repo root run `python -m src.inference.video_inference --input_video VIDEO_PROJECT\input.mp4 --output_video outputs\out.mp4 --weights 8_layers_model\best_model_8_8.pth --layers 8 --device cpu --no_preview`; saves stats `*_stats.txt` and writes side-by-side original|dehazed video.
- Training: paired dataset required (`Dataset/hazy`, `Dataset/clear`); run `python -m src.training.train_dehazenet --device cuda --epochs 50 --batch_size 4` (splits 80/20 if val path omitted); metrics PSNR/SSIM computed per epoch; best checkpoint saved to `weights/dehazenet_{layers}_best.pth`, plots to `weights/plots/`.
- Single-file API (root): `python app.py` starts FastAPI with `/api/upload`, `/api/process`, `/api/status/{job_id}`, `/api/download/{job_id}`, `/api/ws/{job_id}`, `/api/job/{job_id}`; stores uploads in `./uploads`, outputs in `./outputs`; processes videos in background with side-by-side output and JSON stats; .env overrides host/port/device/model.
- Modular backend (web-app/backend): start with `uvicorn app.main:app --reload --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000`; same routes as above under `/api/*`; websockets push progress + base64 previews every few frames; deletes jobs via `/api/job/{job_id}`; serves built frontend from `frontend/dist` if present; mount `/outputs` for downloads.
- Frontend (web-app/frontend): Vite + Tailwind; API base defaults to `http://localhost:8000/api`; run `npm install && npm run dev`; build with `npm run build` before serving from backend. Core components: upload + processing controls in [App.jsx](web-app/frontend/src/App.jsx), comparison view in [SplitScreenComparison.jsx](web-app/frontend/src/components/SplitScreenComparison.jsx), API client in [services/api.js](web-app/frontend/src/services/api.js).
- Docker: from [web-app](web-app) run `docker-compose up` (CPU) or `docker-compose -f docker-compose.gpu.yml up` (GPU) to bring backend+frontend on one port; env variables in compose control `DEVICE`, `ENABLE_FP16`, `DEFAULT_MODEL_LAYERS`, size limits.
- Job processing pipeline (backend): `video_processor.process_video` reads uploads, runs frames through `model_service.infer_frame`, writes side-by-side MP4, tracks status in-memory keyed by `job_id`, emits stats JSON `*_dehazed.json` and progress via WebSocket.
- Verification: `python check_requirements.py` to confirm dependencies/paths; minimal inference smoke test `python -m src.inference.video_inference --input_video sample.mp4 --output_video test.mp4 --device cpu --no_preview` to validate model + OpenCV; health endpoint at `/api/health` reports CUDA availability and paths.
